title: chamaecrista elachistophylla
intro: Chamaecrista elachistophylla, also known as the African sensitive plant, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is traditionally used in African and Asian folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving symptoms of respiratory infections, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer and anti-diabetic properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Chamaecrista elachistophylla are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and optimal usage.
